evals.prompt: Research my top 3 competitors: https://monday.com, https://asana.com, https://clickup.com. I'm building a project management SaaS targeting SMBs. My site is https://example-pm.com. I want to know where I stand and what to prioritize for the next quarter.
evals.expected_output: Full multi-agent pipeline producing a competitive intelligence report. Market classifications for all 4 entities. SEO comparison with keyword visibility across 5-8 queries. Strategic recommendations with a 90-day action plan.
assertions: All 5 agent phases produced output (site profiling, competitor profiling, market classification, SEO comparison, strategic synthesis)
Market segment assigned to each competitor and the user's site
At least 5 keyword queries tested in SEO comparison
Comparison table includes user's site and all 3 competitors
At least 5 strategic recommendations with impact and effort ratings
90-day action plan included with month-by-month breakdown
Executive summary is 3-5 sentences without unexplained jargon
Every recommendation cites specific evidence from agent data
Threat assessment ranks all 3 competitors
Gap analysis categorizes findings into table-stakes, opportunities, and trends

evals.prompt: I'm a smart home installer in Austin, TX. My competitors are https://example-smarthome-a.com and https://example-smarthome-b.com. My site is https://example-smarthome.com. How do I stack up and what should I do differently?
evals.expected_output: Competitive intelligence report tailored for a local service business. SEO comparison includes local search queries with city name. Review counts compared. Recommendations scoped for a small local business.
assertions: Local search queries tested (at least 3 include 'Austin' or geographic qualifier)
Review counts and ratings compared across competitors
Google Business Profile presence checked
Recommendations are actionable for a small business owner (no enterprise-scale suggestions)
Effort estimates realistic for a 1-5 person company
Comparison table includes local-relevant metrics (reviews, service area, local pack presence)
Market segment correctly identifies local services / construction-trades vertical
PageSpeed scores compared across all sites

evals.prompt: I sell email marketing software for e-commerce brands. I don't know who my competitors are. Help me figure out the competitive landscape. My site is https://example-email.com.
evals.expected_output: Skill discovers 3-5 competitors via WebSearch before profiling. Identifies both direct competitors (email tools for e-commerce) and adjacent competitors (general email tools, marketing suites). Full pipeline runs after discovery.
assertions: At least 3 competitors identified via WebSearch before profiling begins
Both direct and adjacent competitors included in the analysis
Competitor URLs presented to user for confirmation before deep analysis
Market classification completed for all discovered competitors
Does not require competitor URLs upfront
Identified competitors are relevant to email marketing for e-commerce

evals.prompt: Quick competitive scan of https://example-competitor.com vs my site https://example-mine.com. Just the highlights.
evals.expected_output: Abbreviated workflow that still runs all agents but produces a shorter output. Executive summary, comparison table, and top 3 recommendations. No 90-day plan. No appendix.
assertions: Output is concise (under 1500 words excluding any raw data)
Still includes comparison data in table format
Still includes at least 3 actionable recommendations
Does not produce 90-day action plan for a quick scan request
Executive summary present and concise
All agent phases still execute (data quality not sacrificed)

evals.prompt: Run competitor research against https://example-a.com, https://example-b.com, https://example-c.com. My site is https://example-user.com.
evals.expected_output: Reads product-marketing-context.md first, does not re-ask intake questions already answered in that file, proceeds directly to agent invocations with context from the file.
assertions: Checks for .agents/product-marketing-context.md or .claude/product-marketing-context.md
Does not re-ask questions answered in the context file (industry, target audience, etc.)
Uses context from the file for industry and business type classification
Proceeds to agent execution without unnecessary intake questions
Full pipeline still runs with all 5 agent phases

files.content:
# Product Marketing Context

## Business
- Name: ExampleCorp
- URL: https://example-user.com
- Industry: SaaS / Developer Tools
- Business model: B2B
- Target: Mid-market engineering teams (50-500 devs)

## Product
- Category: CI/CD platform
- Primary value prop: 10x faster builds with zero configuration
- Pricing: Usage-based, starts at $29/mo

## Audience
- Primary: Engineering managers at mid-size companies
- Secondary: DevOps engineers evaluating build tools
- Key pain: Slow CI pipelines blocking developer productivity
